draft.title: ai workspace
draft.summary: Drafting your prototype from the swipes…
draft.html:
ai workspace

Building your first draft…

facade.hypothesis: Users building AI workspaces prefer high contrast between navigation and work areas to reduce cognitive load
facade.label: Dark sidebar, bright content
facade.acceptImplies: Explore darker navigation patterns with brighter central canvas; test two-tone color systems
facade.rejectImplies: Investigate unified tone palettes or softer contrast boundaries; consider monochromatic or mid-tone designs

facade.hypothesis: Users building an AI workspace want a voice that feels approachable and human-like rather than corporate or robotic
facade.label: Casual, conversational tone
facade.acceptImplies: The brand should use contractions, humor, everyday language, and feel like a knowledgeable friend guiding them through features
facade.rejectImplies: Users prefer a more authoritative, polished, formal tone that signals expertise and stability over warmth

evidence.hypothesis: Users building AI workspaces prefer high contrast between navigation and work areas to reduce cognitive load
evidence.implication: Explore darker navigation patterns with brighter central canvas; test two-tone color systems

facade.hypothesis: Users of AI workspaces prefer snappy, immediate feedback over smooth animations
facade.axisTargeted: Snappy vs. Fluid — does the workspace feel instantly responsive or smoothly eased?
facade.label: Instant response
facade.acceptImplies: Favor crisp, zero-delay interactions: buttons snap, panels appear instantly, no transition delays
facade.rejectImplies: Favor motion-forward design: smooth easing curves, staggered reveals, micro-animations that guide attention

facade.hypothesis: Users building AI workspaces may prefer warm, organic lighting over harsh digital brightness—testing if atmosphere leans toward calm/intimate or energetic/cool
facade.axisTargeted: warm vs cool + calm vs energetic
facade.label: Soft ambient light
facade.acceptImplies: Workspace should feel inviting and human-scaled, with diffused lighting, muted colors, natural materials or textures
facade.rejectImplies: User prefers sharp, bright, clinical aesthetics—likely cool-toned, high-contrast, minimal, sleek digital feel

facade.hypothesis: AI workspace users prefer metrics-first visual hierarchy—prominent KPIs over narrative explanation
facade.label: Dashboard with big numbers
facade.acceptImplies: Workspace should lead with scannable stats, gauges, progress rings. User values at-a-glance status over storytelling.
facade.rejectImplies: Workspace should bury metrics in detail views or prose. User trusts narrative walkthrough and context over raw figures.

axes.label: Whether user prefers minimal/clean aesthetic vs rich/detailed visual design for their workspace
Whether user wants tools always visible vs hidden/revealed on demand in their interface layout
Whether user responds to encouraging/motivational tone vs neutral/professional communication style
Whether user wants bright energetic workspace vs calm focused environment for concentration
Whether user prefers data-heavy dashboards vs simple summaries and key metrics only
Whether user wants instant responsive feedback vs smooth gradual transitions and animations

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers minimal/clean aesthetic vs rich/detailed visual design for their workspace
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Iris

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants tools always visible vs hidden/revealed on demand in their interface layout
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Prism

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user responds to encouraging/motivational tone vs neutral/professional communication style
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Lumen

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants bright energetic workspace vs calm focused environment for concentration
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Aura

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers data-heavy dashboards vs simple summaries and key metrics only
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Facet

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants instant responsive feedback vs smooth gradual transitions and animations
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Echo

facade.hypothesis: Dense layout with persistent navigation and chunked content reduces cognitive load in knowledge work
facade.axisTargeted: Navigation persistence vs. tab-switching; Information density (cards vs. lists)
facade.label: Sidebar + cards
facade.acceptImplies: User values quick context-switching and visual modularity — likely wants to see multiple workspaces/projects at once without mode-switching
facade.rejectImplies: User prefers sequential focus and screen real estate optimization — likely wants full-width views and linear task flows, not parallel browsing

facade.hypothesis: Users building AI workspaces respond better to softer, more approachable visual language (rounded buttons, cards, shadows) than sharp geometric forms, signaling a tool that feels helpful rather than rigid
facade.axisTargeted: Softness vs. Sharpness in component geometry
facade.label: Rounded corners, soft edges
facade.acceptImplies: Explore rounded UI elements, subtle shadows, and organic spacing; test whether softer edges increase perceived approachability in AI tool interfaces
facade.rejectImplies: Users may prefer modern, geometric minimalism with sharp corners and clean lines; explore angular card designs and flat, precise component edges

draft.title: AI Workspace Hub
draft.summary: A focused workspace interface for managing AI tasks, projects, and collaborative workflows. Clean, minimal design with warm neutrals and intuitive navigation.
